title: First Minister wants meeting over US military at Prestwick

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: First Minister John Swinney is looking for a meeting with the UK Government over the use of Prestwick Airport by the US military.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: This article is brought to you by our exclusive subscriber partnership with our sister title USA Today, and has been written by our American colleagues. It does not necessarily reflect the view of The Herald. FIRST Minister John Swinney is seeking a meeting with the UK Government over the use of Prestwick Airport by the US military.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: Earlier this month, Scottish Greens MSP Ross Greer called on Swinney to ban the Americans from using the publicly-owned airport. Critics say that American military planes are stopping off at the South Ayrshire facility on their way to support the US-Israeli offensive against Iran.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – First Minister looks for meeting over US military use of Prestwick Airport
